item.title: China warns of "security backdoor" in Anthropic AI coding tool
item.description: A Chinese industry regulator warned users of a "security backdoor" embedded in versions of U.S. artificial intelligence company Anthropic's coding tool, Claude Code.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran over, calls negotiations "waste of time"
item.description: Oil prices shot up 5% after President Trump said the ceasefire with Iran, in his view, was over, and he called it "a waste of time dealing with them."

item.title: Telegram used to share rape videos and drugging tips, prosecutors say
item.description: The suspects posted videos of their attacks and referred to women as "cars," sedatives as "fuel" and rape as "driving," according to court documents.

item.title: U.S. hits dozens of Iranian targets in retaliatory strikes after ship attacks
item.description: With peace talks on hold, the U.S. said it launched a "series of powerful strikes" on Iran in response to Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: U.S. says China's nuclear weapons buildup "of great concern" after missile test
item.description: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

item.title: California still hasn't released Baby2Baby diaper contract as lawmakers weigh longer public records delays
item.description: The Newsom administration waited 24 days to decide if it would even let Californians see the multi-million-dollar contract with Baby2Baby, a nonprofit linked to First Partner Jennifer Siebel Newsom.

item.title: Unstable building in Midtown forces evacuations, street closures
item.description: The unstable building is the former Pfizer headquarters in Midtown Manhattan that is being converted into apartments.

item.title: Democrats Stevens and El-Sayed spar in tense Michigan Senate debate
item.description: The debate comes days after a third candidate, Mallory McMorrow, suspended her campaign.

item.title: Why do airlines make passengers check their carry-on at the gate?
item.description: Catching a flight with just a carry-on can make travel feel easy. No need to check a bag and all your belongings stay with you. But quite often, that suddenly changes before boarding.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: License of Utah school where Paris Hilton alleged she was abused is revoked
item.description: Utah has revoked the license of a boarding school where Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager, saying the school has "failed to provide applicable health and safety services for clients."

item.title: U.S. launches strikes against Iran over attacks on 3 ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has over the past two days attacked three commercial vessels that were traveling through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Engineering professor on why columns may have buckled in New York City building
item.description: In Midtown Manhattan, buildings had to be evacuated after at least two columns were found buckling at a high-rise construction site. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani also said there are multiple cracks and sagging floors. Magued Iskander, a professor at the NYU Tandon School of Engineering, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect changed clothes before shooting, agent says
item.description: An investigator testified that a video shows Tyler Robinson going over a railing onto a rooftop, crouching down and running to a site overlooking where Charlie Kirk was speaking​.

item.title: Former U.S. ambassador to NATO says Trump lifting Turkey sanctions is "a strange way to do business"
item.description: President Trump touted his growing relationship with Turkey during a two-day NATO summit in Ankara. Former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Ivo Daalder joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Democratic strategist discusses Graham Platner's campaign
item.description: Democratic support is fading for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner after accusations that he sexually assaulted his former girlfriend in 2021. Democratic strategist and CBS News political contributor Joel Payne has more.

item.title: DOJ threatens criminal action against states that allow non-citizens to vote
item.description: The head of the Justice Department's Civil Rights Division sent letters to election officials in all 50 states threatening criminal action if they knowingly allow non-U.S. citizens to vote.

item.title: FDA investigating infant botulism outbreak linked to recalled formula
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration is investigating a multi-state outbreak of infant botulism linked to baby formula. Infant botulism is a rare but serious illness caused when babies consume bacteria with spores that produce a toxin in the gut. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: New video shows alleged Charlie Kirk shooting suspect before and after incident
item.description: Prosecutors allege that Tyler Robinson shot conservative activist Charlie Kirk while he was speaking at an event for Turning Point USA. In court on Tuesday, a judge allowed new surveillance footage from the day the conservative activist was shot and killed.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Former Make-A-Wish kid now inspires those like him: "There is a light at the end of the tunnel"
item.description: Just a couple of weeks after Kai Rackley got his wings as a first officer for United Airlines, he got a chance to meet a group of kids a lot like he used to be. Tony Dokoupil has the story about the magic of the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

item.title: Details from Charlie Kirk murder suspect's pretrial hearing in Utah
item.description: Prosecutors revealed new video from the moments before and after the shooting of conservative activist Charlie Kirk. Carter Evans reports from Utah with details on the pretrial hearing there on Tuesday.

item.title: What happens when a lawmaker takes an extended absence?
item.description: Senate GOP leaders say they've spoken to Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell, who has been hospitalized for more than three weeks. USA Today Washington bureau chief Susan Page joins to discuss.

item.title: Graham Platner faces tight deadline in Maine Senate race amid calls to drop out
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to drop out of the Maine Senate race over a sexual assault allegation he denies. If Platner does decide to end his campaign, he needs to do so by July 13 or he'll remain the Democratic nominee. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports from Maine.

item.title: World Cup latest as U.S. men fall, Argentina makes a comeback
item.description: The World Cup is down to what college basketball fans call the "elite eight," and it doesn't include the U.S. men's national team after Monday night's collapse against Belgium. But the action is not over. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: 7/7: CBS Evening News
item.description: Unstable New York City building forces evacuations; heavy rains bring severe flooding to East Coast.

item.title: Fetterman tells those who backed Platner to "sit it out" on a possible replacement
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman condemned his colleagues who continued backing Graham Platner amid a string of scandals.

item.title: Federal court blocks Florida law banning "woke" instruction in universities
item.description: A 2-1 ruling from the 11th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals found Florida's assertion it could control the speech of its employees to be "a breathtaking assertion of power to ban unpopular ideas from public discourse in the very places the state's own statutes recognize as centers of inquiry."

item.title: Ford recalls 177,000 vehicles over faulty wiper, drivetrain and other issues
item.description: A slew of Ford recalls affects some new and used Mustang, Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid and Explorer Hybrid vehicles, according to a federal safety watchdog.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel expected to say Israel needs "significant changes" in speech
item.description: Rahm Emanuel​ is viewed as a potential Democratic presidential candidate and has been a longtime defender of Israel.
